desc: Malay municipality includes Boracay access points and nearby coastal areas, combining beach time, water activities, and island transfers.
hls: Gateway to Boracay via Caticlan with short boat transfers
White Beach sunsets, calm-water swimming, and long sandy walks
Bulabog Beach wind season for kiteboarding and watersports
Island-hopping to coves, snorkeling spots, and cliff viewpoints
Lively dining and nightlife balanced by quieter shoulder months
Wide range of stays from hostels to upscale beachfront resorts

todo.name: Sunrise walk on White Beach
todo.desc: Start early before crowds build, then swim while sea conditions are usually calmer.

todo.name: Try kitesurfing at Bulabog Beach
todo.desc: Book a beginner lesson in windy season months when side-onshore conditions are most reliable.

todo.name: Join a Crocodile Island boat trip
todo.desc: Take a half-day island-hopping route with guided reef stops and gear included.

todo.name: Puka Beach and north-coast chill day
todo.desc: Head north for a less built-up shoreline and slower pace away from central stations.

todo.name: Ride a traditional paraw
todo.desc: Sail at golden hour for skyline views, then return for dinner on the beachfront path.

todo.name: Sample D'Mall and Station 2 eateries
todo.desc: Plan a casual food crawl mixing Filipino grills, seafood, and late-night dessert spots.

prac.visa: Check latest entry requirements based on your passport before booking.
alerts: Southwest monsoon (roughly June to October) can bring rough seas, rain interruptions, and occasional canceled boat activities.
Christmas, Lunar New Year, Holy Week, and long Philippine holiday weekends push room rates up sharply.
Caticlan jetty queues and terminal fees add transfer time; allow buffer when connecting to flights.
Beach management rules (smoking/drinking zones, closures for weather) are enforced and can affect plans.

wifi.note: Most Boracay hotels, cafes, and cowork-friendly spots offer solid fiber or 5G internet, though brownouts and weather can cause brief drops during stormy months.

prac.sim: Buy an eSIM/local SIM in arrival city for best coverage and data value.
prac.safety: Use standard travel precautions and follow local weather/sea-condition guidance.
prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; most UK itineraries connect via Manila or Cebu, then continue to Caticlan (MPH) or Kalibo (KLO).
